resources.string: The launcher basic tutorial has been released. Everyone is welcome to come and support it!
Open the Bilibili tutorial video for first-time setup and usage guidance.
Controls Steam Workshop mod downloads in the market.
How many Workshop mods may download at the same time.
Controls Workshop chunk download concurrency for each mod.
Uses Watt Toolkit routes for Steam Workshop, Store/CDN, and preview image requests.
Controls Steam Workshop browse/search language and SteamPipe metadata language.
Automatically imports downloaded Workshop jar mods. If disabled, downloaded jars stay in the Workshop card until you import them manually.
Not configured. Translation needs AppID and API Key.
Used by the market detail translate button. Fill both AppID and API Key from Baidu AI text translation. Leave both blank and save to clear them.
Saved current fields, but translation requires both AppID and API Key.
Complete credentials are saved. Saving changes will overwrite the local values. Save with both fields blank to clear them.
Fill both AppID and API Key for Baidu AI text translation. Save with both fields blank to clear the local configuration.
Baidu AI text translation test succeeded.
Baidu AI text translation test failed. Please try again later.
Removes cached Workshop mod cover images stored on this device. Covers will be downloaded again when shown.
Game process exited unexpectedly, exit code %1$d.
Game process crashed and exited due to signal %1$d.
OutOfMemoryError detected (JVM heap exhausted). The game automatically returned to the launcher. JVM heap size may be too large or too small; adjust the JVM heap max in Settings.

The previous game session ended abnormally and did not return a crash report before the launcher restarted. This is very likely caused by the device being under too much pressure and unable to handle an overly large mod setup. Reduce the number of mods and try again.

This run peaked at about %1$d / %2$d MB of JVM heap (%3$d%%).\n\nIf you often see stutter or exits when many mods are enabled, consider raising the JVM heap max in Settings. The current heap max is %4$d MB; a reasonable next step is %5$d MB.

This run peaked at about %1$d / %2$d MB of JVM heap (%3$d%%).\n\nHeap pressure was high. The current heap max is already %4$d MB, so if stutter or exits continue, you may need to trim enabled mods or other memory-heavy content.
No available app can share the crash report.

It is recommended to troubleshoot the issue first. You can hand the logs to AI analysis to confirm whether the problem comes from the launcher itself or from a mod. If you can confirm that the launcher is the problem, you can use the feedback page to report it to the developer.
This run likely exhausted JVM heap memory. Before retrying, consider opening recovery settings and raising the JVM heap max, or trim recently enabled mods if memory pressure stays high.
Do not leave the app, switch to another app, or let the system clean up this app during the patch process. Keep the launcher in the foreground.
You can retry immediately, or open recovery settings first before starting again. The crash text below supports selection and copying, and is also more convenient for long screenshots.
Only the captured error is shown below. Export the logs if you need more detailed diagnostics.
Prompt copied. Paste it to AI for analysis.

Please help me analyze the following Slay the Spire Android Mod launcher crash log.

This launcher runs Slay the Spire and its mods on Android devices. The game is based on Java / LibGDX, and mods are usually loaded through the ModTheSpire, BaseMod, and StSLib ecosystem. The launcher prepares the runtime environment, loads resources, configures JVM/runtime parameters, manages mods, and collects crash logs.

The player crashed while playing modded Slay the Spire on Android and copied the following information from the crash page.

Please determine whether the crash is more likely caused by the launcher, the base game, a specific mod, mod conflicts, device environment, permissions, resource files, graphics/audio drivers, or configuration. Explain in language the player can understand:
1. What is the most likely cause;
2. What evidence supports that conclusion, especially key errors, class names, method names, mod names, or resource paths in the log;
3. What the player can try to fix it;
4. If it cannot be determined, what additional information is needed.

Do not only repeat the log. Try to provide a conclusion, evidence, and next steps.

Error information:
%1$s
These switches do not modify already imported mod jars. Changes apply to later launches/runtime behavior, so no mod re-import is needed.
These switches only affect mods imported from now on. Existing jars are not retroactively rewritten, so re-import the relevant mod if you want the patch to apply.

Newly imported mods are patched offline during import: atlas filter lines containing mipmap are rewritten to Linear,Linear. Keeping this enabled gives higher compatibility; if disabled, new imports will not be patched. Example fix: black blocks on the Ring of Fate main menu.

This is no longer a global default switch. Atlas offline pre-downscale is opt-in per import: when enabled in the import prompt or wizard, likely Spine or skeletal-animation atlas pages are resampled and matching atlas coordinate fields are rewritten. This can lower runtime memory spikes for heavy animation mods, but may soften art or affect unusual atlas layouts. Existing imported jars are not changed.

Controls the target max edge used by import-time atlas offline pre-downscale. Smaller targets are more aggressive and save more memory, but they also blur close-up character art more. This only affects newly imported mods.
A 4096x4096 atlas page would be resampled to about %1$dx%2$d.

During mod import, jars are automatically patched when ModTheSpire.json is incorrectly packaged under an outer folder (for example, Fabricate/ModTheSpire.json): it is rewritten to the jar root. Keeping this enabled gives higher compatibility; if disabled, newly imported mods are not patched. A dialog is shown when auto-fix happens.

When enabled, importing frierenmod automatically patches FrierenMod/utils/AntiPirateHelper.antiPirate() to remove the desktop anti-piracy dialog path that blocks Android startup at 97%. If disabled, newly imported FrierenMod jars are left untouched.

When enabled, importing downfall automatically patches merchant drawX to use a Settings.WIDTH/xScale-aware position, scales Hexaghost MyBody render offsets with Settings.scale, and reanchors the boss mechanic popup to the active boss so it renders to the right of the boss. This targets wide-screen drift and boss-obscuring UI on mobile. If disabled, newly imported Downfall jars are left untouched.

When enabled, importing vupshionmod automatically patches VUPShionMod/ui/WebButton to remove the Steam workshop subscription gate that can break main-menu background initialization. If disabled, newly imported VUPShionMod jars are left untouched.

When enabled, shaders launched through the patched desktop jar automatically strip desktop GLSL version headers and fragment shaders receive missing default precision declarations on GLES/ANGLE paths, reducing errors like \"shader version mismatch\" and \"no default precision defined for variable\". This is a runtime compatibility switch and takes effect on next launch.

Used to fix black blocks caused by dynamically created runtime textures. If you see missing materials/textures, consider enabling it. Pros: continues fixing black blocks not covered during import-time patching. Cons: increases startup CPU/GPU overhead from managed texture scanning, and runtime Linear/Clamp fallback may make some textures blurrier. Default is OFF to prioritize performance.

When enabled, main-menu character previews reuse existing character instances when safe, and cache the button/portrait textures used by modded character options to avoid rebuilding and reloading them every time you return to the main menu. This reduces return-to-menu stutter and makes related diagnostics easier to read. If you are investigating character-select compatibility regressions, temporarily disable it to keep behavior closer to the original mod stack.

When enabled, the launcher stops exposing native touchscreen semantics globally to mods and only re-enables verified vanilla touchscreen confirm flows such as the shop and boss relic confirm paths, while keeping the touch indicator visible. This reduces modded screens getting trapped in untested touchscreen state machines. Disable it if you want to restore the original global native touchscreen semantics. This is a runtime compatibility switch and takes effect on the next launch.

Read-only status. This runtime path can be active in Legacy GPU guardian mode or when Guardian pressure downscale is enabled in performance settings. It resamples large file-backed texture uploads under pressure and does not rewrite mod jars. Changes apply on the next launch.
Locked. This legacy texture reclaim path is enabled only when GPU resource guardian is set to Legacy.

Controls the unified low-overhead GPU resource guard for tracked textures and FrameBuffers. Safe mode only reads counters in normal frames and uses bounded slice reclaim under sustained pressure. This takes effect on the next launch.
Disable guardian and leave resource management to the game/mod stack. Legacy resource-management switches are locked off.
Default. Monitor aggregate tracked bytes with bounded texture/FBO reclaim only after sustained pressure.
Use tighter pressure thresholds and reclaim budgets for severe mod-related GPU memory growth. May cause more rebuild/restore stutter.
Safe-mode behavior with additional diagnostic logging for short investigations. Do not leave this on for normal play.
Used only while runtime texture pressure downscale is active. x2 is the safest; x3/x4 save more memory but blur more. Changes apply on the next launch and do not rewrite mod jars.
A 2048x2048 runtime texture uploads at about %1$dx%2$d.

When enabled, mipmap-dependent min filters are downgraded to Linear at runtime to avoid black blocks caused by incomplete mip chains in some mods. Example fix: Ring of Fate character models turning into black blocks.

When enabled, the Blue Archive Hina 3D helper keeps the original logical camera scale while switching to a mobile-safe offscreen FrameBuffer and a simpler final blit path. This fixes blank or incorrectly scaled Hina character renders on Android-compatible runtimes. Disable it if you need the original mod behavior for comparison. This takes effect on the next launch.

When enabled, FrameBuffer formats that cannot be used as color attachments on GLES (Alpha/Intensity/LuminanceAlpha) are automatically upgraded to RGBA8888 to avoid startup crashes such as \"frame buffer could not be constructed: incomplete attachment\". Disabling this is closer to original mod behavior, but may reintroduce startup crashes. If your Haberdashery relic display mod has issues, consider enabling this option.
Locked. This legacy global FBO manager is enabled only when GPU resource guardian is set to Legacy.
Disabled. This legacy idle FBO reclaim path is kept visible for compatibility context, but it is no longer user-toggleable.

Read-only status. FBO pressure downscale can be active in Legacy GPU guardian mode or when Guardian pressure downscale is enabled in performance settings. It lowers large offscreen FrameBuffer allocation resolution under pressure and does not rewrite mod jars. Changes apply on the next launch.

Choose which material classes may be downscaled. These switches do not enable downscaling by themselves; they only limit the import-time downscale prompt/wizard and the runtime pressure downscale path.
Allow runtime pressure downscale for direct Texture uploads that are not loaded through TextureAtlas or Spine stacks.
Caps libGDX TextureAtlas page uploads at the selected clarity. Lower values save more GPU memory and may blur atlas-backed art. Changes apply on the next launch.
Most aggressive. Caps atlas pages near 1280x720 for low-memory runs.
Sharper but uses more GPU memory. Caps atlas pages near 2560x1440.
Allow runtime pressure downscale for textures loaded through Spine or skeletal-animation stacks. Disabled by default because animation attachments can be sensitive to texture metadata.
Allow runtime pressure downscale for large offscreen FrameBuffers when the runtime FBO pressure path is active. This can blur offscreen effects but does not rewrite mod jars.
Allow import-time offline downscale for atlas pages whose atlas appears to belong to Spine or skeletal animation. This is the default import-time target.
Allow import-time offline downscale for atlas pages whose atlas is not recognized as Spine or skeletal animation. Disabled by default because generic atlas regions are often drawn by pixel size.
Controls whether the touch mouse floating window is shown in game.
Tap the floating mouse to open the shortcut menu; use the menu button to switch left/right click.
Tap the floating mouse to switch left/right click; long-press to open the shortcut menu.
Choose how the floating mouse opens shortcuts and switches left/right click.
Touch long-press as right click: Enabled
Touch long-press as right click: Disabled
When enabled, holding the game surface for 0.5 seconds sends one right-click. Moving your finger before then cancels the right-click gesture.

When enabled, the floating menu keyboard button opens a built-in full-width keyboard instead of the system keyboard. Disable this if you need the system IME again. Takes effect the next time the game launches.
Controls launcher and in-game overlay vibration feedback, including taps, switches, sliders, and mod toggles.
Compendium upgrade toggle touch fix: Enabled
Compendium upgrade toggle touch fix: Disabled
In touchscreen mode, enlarges the Compendium card popup "View Upgrades" tap target and applies the toggle on touch-down so it is easier to hit. Takes effect the next time the game launches.
SurfaceView (default, faster but can stutter on some systems)
TextureView (compatibility fallback, usually steadier but heavier)

Controls which Android view hosts the game surface. Changes take effect the next time the game launches. If SurfaceView stutters on some ROMs or becomes smoother when screen recording is enabled, try TextureView.
Game is ready. Tap Close loading overlay to enter.
Launcher components already up to date
Runtime pack already up to date
MTS progress bridge unavailable, using console progress...
MTS startup failed: Steam installation not found
Forced JVM crash requested for diagnostics
The launcher lost contact with the startup preparation process before launch could continue.

This imported desktop-1.0.jar was already rewritten by an older launcher build and can't be upgraded in place to the newer compatibility patch flow.

Open Settings and re-import the original, unmodified desktop-1.0.jar before launching again.
If you only have the current file, copy a fresh jar from your game install.
JVM exited before the game became interactive
JVM exited before the game became interactive (%1$s)
Check out my other project, Workshop Mod Downloader, which lets you browse and download mods. You will need your own proxy setup or a Steam accelerator when using it.\n\nProject URL: %1$s
Some mod notices have not been read
These enabled mods still have unread notices:
%1$d more enabled mods are not shown here.
It is recommended to open the circled exclamation mark on each mod card and read the notice first. You can cancel this launch, or continue anyway.

The currently enabled mods total about %1$s. Too many large mods are very likely to put excessive pressure on this device.
Do you still want to continue?
The game process is still running. Return to that session or wait for it to exit before starting again.
The previous game process did not exit fully. Please wait a moment and try again.
Failed to save launcher settings before launch. Please try again.
Patched game runtime jar required to boot Slay the Spire on Android.
Primary mod loader jar used to launch the game with mods.
Core prerequisite mod required by most modded Slay the Spire setups.
Shared library prerequisite used by many mods.
Bundled runtime compat mod that caches expensive Duelist Guarded dynamic-value lookups and shortcuts expensive Duelist tribute/summon base-value rendering paths on mobile.
No mods yet. Download from Workshop, then import with the bottom-left button.
The launcher can\'t access %1$s right now. Mods may appear empty, and launch/export actions are temporarily disabled.
Fully close the launcher and reopen it. If it still fails, restart the phone and disable battery optimization/background cleaning for this app.

Try this:
1. Fully close the launcher and reopen it. Do not restore it from recent apps.
2. If it still fails, restart the phone.
3. Add the launcher to your battery/background whitelist and disable cleaner apps.
Change the folder used for mod categories
Delete %1$d selected mods? This cannot be undone.
The mod notice list was updated. Mods with a circled exclamation mark now have notices you can open from their cards.
Lower numbers load earlier. Prerequisites of the current mod are automatically moved before this mod.
Current effective priority: #%1$d (inherited from dependent mods)
Explicit priority: #%1$d. Current effective priority: #%2$d because another earlier mod depends on it.
No explicit priority is set. The default priority is #11.
Built-in core components can\'t be imported
Move this mod back to the previous folder automatically
Rename this mod to the old file name automatically

VUPShionMod is extremely well made!

The developer also provided separate authorization for launcher support so this mod can run on the launcher.

If you are able, please subscribe to the mod on Steam Workshop or support the author on Afdian. Thank you very much!!
Compressed archive detected. Extract it and import the .jar inside.
The following files couldn\'t be parsed as importable mods:

Make sure you selected an actual mod jar and that it still contains a readable ModTheSpire.json inside the jar. Non-mod jars, damaged jars, or jars repacked incorrectly from archives can\'t be imported.
The following files are compressed archives and can\'t be imported directly as mods:
Extract the archive first, then import the .jar mod files inside.
The following mods have modids that duplicate already imported mods or other mods in this selection:
Choosing "%1$s" cancels this import; choosing "%2$s" continues and lets later imported files replace earlier files with the same modid; choosing "%3$s" continues and keeps both files.
The following built-in core components were blocked from import:
BaseMod, StSLib, and ModTheSpire are bundled and managed by the launcher. Do not import them manually.
No atlas files needed offline patching in this import.
The following mods had atlas filter settings patched during import:
Patched atlas files: %1$d, replaced filter lines: %2$d
Patch rule: rewrite filter lines containing mipmap to Linear,Linear.
No atlas page images needed offline preprocessing in this import.
The following mods had oversized atlas page images preprocessed during import:
Rewritten atlas files: %1$d, downscaled page images: %2$d, estimated runtime texture memory saved: %3$s
Patch rule: eligible page images in likely Spine atlases are resampled according to the selected offline pre-downscale strategy, and the related atlas coordinate fields are rewritten to match.
The following mods would trigger offline atlas pre-downscaling during import:
Candidate atlas files: %1$d, candidate page images: %2$d, estimated runtime texture memory saved: %3$s
Pros: 1. Lowers runtime memory spikes for heavy animation mods 2. Reduces crashes or force-kills caused by GPU memory pressure under the compatibility layer.
Cons: 1. Large character art and details may become noticeably blurry 2. Already imported mods are not automatically restored to the original images; restoring requires re-importing.

Before continuing the import, choose the downscale strategy and level below. The actual number of processed page images may differ by strategy. If your device has less memory (12 GB or below), downscaling by resolution is recommended. If your device has enough memory, you can try downscaling by percentage so the image quality loss is less noticeable.
Candidate atlas pages larger than %2$dpx are resampled to %1$d%% of their original size.
Only candidate atlas pages larger than %1$dpx are changed, and each changed page is capped to %1$dpx on its longest edge.
No ModTheSpire.json root-path issues were found in this import.
The following mods had ModTheSpire.json root paths auto-fixed during import:
Patch rule: flatten "outer-folder/ModTheSpire.json" into the jar root.
No FrierenMod startup-blocking logic needed patching in this import.
The following mods had FrierenMod startup-blocking logic auto-patched during import:
Removed the desktop popup block in FrierenMod/utils/AntiPirateHelper.antiPirate()
Patch rule: replace antiPirate() with a no-op so Android startup doesn\'t stall at 97%.
No Downfall layout/UI fixes needed patching in this import.
The following mods had Downfall layout/UI fixes auto-patched during import:
Patched classes: %1$d, merchant position classes: %2$d, Hexaghost body classes: %3$d, boss mechanic panel classes: %4$d

Patch rule: merchant drawX now uses Settings.WIDTH/xScale-aware positioning, Hexaghost MyBody offset constants scale with Settings.scale, and BossMechanicDisplayPanel updates its x/y/width from the current boss hitbox so the popup stays to the right of the boss.
No VUPShionMod Steam-gate fixes needed patching in this import.
The following mods had VUPShionMod startup compatibility logic auto-patched during import:
Removed the Steam workshop subscription guard in VUPShionMod/ui/WebButton so the main-menu background can initialize without Steam ownership checks
Patch rule: strip the SteamApps.isSubscribedApp(646570) gate from VUPShionMod/ui/WebButton while leaving the rest of the class behavior intact.
No JacketNoAnoKo shader compatibility fixes needed patching in this import.
The following mods had JacketNoAnoKo shader compatibility logic auto-patched during import:
Patched shader files: %1$d, removed desktop #version directives: %2$d, inserted fragment precision blocks: %3$d

Patch rule: rewrite shader files in JacketNoAnoKo shader resource folders so desktop GLSL #version 120 headers are stripped, and fragment shaders missing default precision receive a GLES-compatible precision block.
Reading selected files and building an import plan.
Choose how duplicate mod IDs should be handled before import starts.
When replacing, reuse previous file name
Review compatibility patches that can be applied during import.
Compressed archives must be extracted before importing mod jars.
Selected file is not a readable mod jar.
ModTheSpire is a launcher-managed core component.
ModTheSpire.json did not contain a usable modid.
Remove duplicate zip entries while keeping the first copy.
Move a nested ModTheSpire.json package root to the jar root.
Manifest was already at jar root.
Rewrite mipmap atlas filters to Linear,Linear.
No mipmap atlas filters needed patching.
Optionally resample oversized Spine atlas page images before import.
No atlas pages matched the selected downscale strategy.
Remove FrierenMod desktop anti-piracy startup block on Android.
FrierenMod anti-piracy method did not need patching.
Patch Downfall mobile layout and boss UI compatibility points.
Downfall layout compatibility did not need patching.
Remove Steam ownership startup gate and related fragile paths.
VUPShionMod startup compatibility did not need patching.
Rewrite desktop shader headers for GLES compatibility.
JacketNoAnoKo shader compatibility did not need patching.
Reading and checking the import file. Please wait...
Could not import file from this app

The launcher was opened from another app, but that app did not pass a readable jar file in a supported Android format.\n\nTry opening the jar from a file manager, or save the file locally first and then import it from the launcher.
The launcher detected that you opened the base game jar from another app and completed the normal desktop-1.0.jar import flow.\n\nSource file: %1$s
Do not touch this when the game has no problems
Changes the Slay the Spire profile name. Takes effect after you enter the game again.
Leave it blank to restore the default name: player.
Choose whether the launcher follows the system appearance or always uses light or dark mode.
Choose the launcher accent color. The same color palette is automatically adapted for light and dark mode.
When enabled, the launcher checks for the latest release on startup. If no update is found, it stays silent.
Update checks, feedback issue browsing, mod suggestions, and other GitHub-backed downloads all use this selected source or direct-access strategy.
No update check has run yet.
You are already on the latest version.
Direct downloads come from GitHub Releases and may not have stable speeds. If you want to support development, use Quark Download instead; the developer earns revenue from each download.
Tower 2 mobile mod loader by wsdx233. Check it out here:
1. Technical ideas from the Amethyst-android project.
2. Design ideas from this project predecessor (mobile build).
3. Help from Butterfly Is Larva and friends in their fan group.
Many thanks to all friends above for their hard work.
Official releases are distributed only through non-throttled cloud storage or Github Release.
If you downloaded this project from other sources, it is unauthorized third-party redistribution.
Follow official release channels for the latest builds and announcements.
欢迎加入官方互助交流QQ群：
This sheet tracks mods that are currently unplayable and their fix status. You can directly edit the sheet to add unplayable mod details and help us finish compatibility fixes faster.
Capture system logcat during game sessions after launch and include it in exported diagnostics archives. Disabled by default.
Capture launcher-related logcat while the launcher process is alive and include it in exported diagnostics archives. Enabled by default.
A feedback issue you follow has a new reply. Open your followed issues to check progress.
%1$d feedback issues you follow have new replies. Open your followed issues to check progress.
The mod directory is currently unavailable due to an OS issue. Restarting the phone is recommended.
A game session is still running
This is expected: you triggered "Back button: exit to launcher".\n\nIf you do not want this behavior, change Back button behavior to "Esc" or "No action" in Settings.
Disable mods that depend on it first: %1$s
Mod is missing or already deleted: %1$s
The mod file is missing and cannot be exported.
The mod file is missing and cannot be shared.
No available app can share this mod.
The mod is missing and cannot be renamed.
Applied file names as aliases for %1$d mod(s).

The old "Show file name" feature has been removed. You can now rename mods without enabling file-name display. Because you may have renamed multiple mods through that feature before, do you want to automatically adjust all mod names to their file names?
Failed to update mod enabled state: %1$s
Unable to resolve the mod identity, so load priority could not be updated.
Removed the explicit priority, but this mod still loads at #%1$d because another earlier mod depends on it.
Restored the default load order for "%1$s"
Enabled "%1$s", but these prerequisites are missing:
Batch enable finished, but these prerequisites are missing:
Import the missing prerequisites first, or the game may fail to start correctly.
Some mods could not be disabled
Deleted %1$d mods, %2$d missing or already deleted
Multiple enabled mods share the same modid:
Leave only one enabled mod for each duplicated modid before starting the game.
The following enabled mods cannot be parsed by ModTheSpire at launch:
Check whether these mods have a ModTheSpire.json that matches the expected ModTheSpire format.
If these jars were unpacked and repacked from archives, import the original jars again instead.

An enabled Ram Saver mod was detected while Global texture residency manager is on.\n\nTo avoid texture lifecycle conflicts, this launch will automatically skip texture residency reclaim and leave Ram Saver to manage those textures.
Tutorial, appearance, updates, and setup guide
Rendering, input, interaction, resources, and files
Steam Cloud saves and market download behavior
Issue reports, save/resource packages, and exported logs
Project links, contributors, credits, and notices
Re-enter the first-run setup flow and review the recommended basic launcher settings.
Start by choosing the theme mode and accent color for %1$s.
Adjust visual clarity, frame pacing, touchscreen behavior, and the larger UI and text options before your first run.
Configure the default interaction style, soft keyboard behavior, and the floating mouse controls that %1$s should use.
Choose whether %1$s checks for updates automatically and which mirror source it prefers.
Sign in to Steam if needed, then choose whether %1$s should use local saves or Steam Cloud saves.
Welcome to the SlayTheAmethyst launcher. To get the best experience, take a moment to complete some basic setup by following this guide.
To adapt the game for mobile, the launcher applies several changes to improve readability and the overall play experience. Adjust these behaviors to match your preference.
When enabled, a black margin is shown on the left side to keep full-screen camera cutouts from covering game content, which can otherwise make some mod controls impossible to tap.
When enabled, a black margin is shown on the right side so the top-right settings area remains tappable on some full-screen devices.
Choose how game touch input is exposed. Hybrid mode is recommended for modded play; mobile mode restores the original global touchscreen behavior.
When enabled, the game uses a larger UI that is easier to tap, but it also changes some visual effects. Choose based on your own preference.
Controls text size. The original game uses a scale of 1, and text that is too small can be hard to read on a phone.
Controls some special interactions, such as how mods that require keyboard and mouse should be operated on a phone.
Choose whether tapping opens the shortcut menu or switches left/right click. The toggle mode keeps long-press for the shortcut menu.
When enabled, holding the game surface for 0.5 seconds sends one right-click, then ends the long-press gesture. If you move your finger first, the gesture stays as normal left-click/drag input.
Controls whether the floating mouse window is shown. When enabled, a floating window appears to provide mouse and keyboard actions.

The launcher includes built-in update checking, and this page configures the update strategy. The launcher is still actively maintained, and each update aims to improve the experience, so staying on the latest version is recommended.
A mirror source is needed to avoid GitHub access issues.
If the current mirror source cannot be reached, switch it in Settings.

The launcher includes built-in cloud save support so progress can move between phone and PC. If you own Slay the Spire on Steam, sign in first and then use the launcher cloud button to check and sync saves.
Intelligently chooses the fastest Steam network route to speed up cloud save synchronization. If sign-in or sync fails, turn this off and use an accelerator instead.
After logging out, cloud saves will be unavailable. You can sign in again later.
Download optional native libraries such as `gdx-video`.

The launcher fetches optional native libraries from the SlayTheAmethystResource market repository through the currently selected mirror source. Installed libraries are saved in a separate directory, so app updates will not overwrite them.
No native library packages are currently available from the market metadata.
Advanced rendering, compatibility patches, and diagnostic switches. Most players should not need to change these.
Reset normal and developer launcher options. Steam Cloud login, active save profile, sync blacklist and records, imported files, mods, and saves are kept.
This resets normal and developer launcher options to their defaults. Steam Cloud login, active save profile, sync blacklist and records, imported files, mods, and saves will not be cleared.

Steam Cloud integration for manual Phase 2 sync. This build supports account login, manifest refresh, manifest viewing, upload planning/conflict review, manual upload of safe local-only changes, full pull for `preferences/` and `saves/`, and the launcher cloud button workflow for checking, syncing, and resolving conflicts.
Access Steam through forwarding rules for better speed. If Steam cannot be accessed normally, turn this off and enable an accelerator.
Switch between independent local saves and Steam Cloud saves. Use the launcher cloud button to check status, sync changes, and resolve conflicts.
Sign in to enable Steam Cloud sync.
Choose which save profile the launcher uses. Independent saves are kept separate from the Steam Cloud profile.
Use the local saves from before Steam sign-in and do not sync with Steam Cloud.
Use the Steam Cloud profile for supported saves and preferences, then manage checks, sync, and conflicts from the launcher cloud button.
Log in to Steam before selecting Steam Cloud saves.
Blacklisted local files stay on this device only. They are excluded from Steam Cloud upload and download, and a full pull keeps the local copy.
Gameplay preference file. Includes touchscreen input and other device-side preferences.
Force current independent saves over Steam Cloud
Use the current independent saves to overwrite Steam Cloud saves. After other devices sync, they will use these saves, and the current Steam Cloud saves will be lost.
Force current independent saves over Steam Cloud
The launcher will first back up both the current Steam Cloud saves and independent saves to Downloads/SlayTheAmethystBackup/, then overwrite Steam Cloud with the current independent saves.
Compare current local files against the last sync baseline and show upload candidates, conflicts, and remote-only changes.
Refresh the manifest, compare against the last sync baseline, and upload only safe local-only changes. Phase 2 never deletes Steam Cloud files.
Back up local saves first, then replace local `preferences/` and `saves/` with the current Steam Cloud manifest.
Remove the stored account name, refresh token, guard data, and cached manifest.
The launcher will not write your password to local storage or upload credentials to a server.
The launcher only stores the refresh token and guard data after a successful login.
This removes the saved Steam Cloud token and guard data from this device. You can log in again later.

This will refresh the latest Steam Cloud manifest, download all supported files, back up your current saves to Downloads, and then replace local `preferences/` and `saves/`. Local extra files in those two folders will be removed.
This will upload %1$d local file(s) to Steam Cloud (%2$s total). Only local-only changes are uploaded; remote deletions are never issued in Phase 2.
%1$d remote-only change(s) will remain untouched. Pull first if you want those changes locally.
Baseline: %1$s | Upload candidates: %2$d (%3$s) | Conflicts: %4$d | Remote-only changes: %5$d
This file already exists locally and remotely, but no sync baseline is saved yet. Complete a full pull once before uploading over it.
Both the local file and the Steam Cloud file changed since the last sync baseline.
Steam Cloud added this file since the last sync baseline.
Steam Cloud changed this file since the last sync baseline.
Steam Cloud deleted this file since the last sync baseline.
Enter a new Steam Guard 2FA code
Enter a new Steam email code
Approve the Steam login on your trusted device, then continue.
Enter the current 2FA code from Steam Guard.
Enter the email code sent by Steam.
Enter the email code sent by Steam to %1$s.
That code was not accepted. Enter the latest code and try again.
Complete this step within about %1$d seconds, or Steam may close the login connection.
Backing up saves and overwriting Steam Cloud…
Log in to Steam before using Steam Cloud saves.
No independent save profile is available yet.
Independent saves overwrote Steam Cloud. Uploaded %1$d file(s), deleted %2$d cloud file(s). Backups are in %3$s.
Failed to overwrite Steam Cloud with independent saves: %1$s

The launcher switched to Steam Cloud saves automatically. Saves from before enabling this feature were preserved locally as Independent saves. You can switch between them in Settings > Cloud save settings.
Steam Cloud login succeeded, but the launcher could not switch to Steam Cloud saves automatically: %1$s. You can change it manually in Settings > Cloud save settings.
Steam Guard verification waited too long. Steam closed the login connection; sign in again and enter the latest code or approve the prompt quickly.
No cached Steam Cloud manifest is available yet.
Failed to build the Steam Cloud upload plan: %1$s
Steam Cloud manual upload completed: %1$d files uploaded.
No local-only Steam Cloud changes are ready to upload.
Steam Cloud upload blocked: %1$d conflict(s) require manual review.
Steam Cloud full pull completed: %1$d files applied.
Steam Cloud is not logged in yet.
No sync baseline yet. Complete a full pull once before uploading over existing cloud files.
Steam Cloud is ready. Tap to check.
Steam Cloud is up to date. Tap to check again.
Steam Cloud has direct save conflicts, tap to resolve.
Steam Cloud sync in progress. Tap to view progress.
Steam Cloud check failed. Tap to retry.
Tap Check again to compare local saves with the latest Steam Cloud state.
Local saves and Steam Cloud are already in sync.
Comparing local saves against the latest Steam Cloud state.
Conflict details are temporarily unavailable. Refresh once and try again.
Steam Cloud check failed. Retry to reconnect.
Steam Cloud sync failed, so your latest cloud progress could not be verified. Starting now uses the current local saves and may leave cloud progress out of date until the next successful sync.
Check cancelled. Your current saves are not synced.
Steam Cloud upload cancelled. Your current saves are not synced.
Direct conflicts were found. Choose which side should win for the conflicting save files.
Use local progress: overwrite the cloud save with the current local save.
Use cloud progress: back up current local saves first, then replace local `preferences/` and `saves/` with Steam Cloud.

This will make Steam Cloud match this device. Conflicting cloud files can be overwritten, and cloud-only files that are not present locally can be removed.

Local changes: %1$d
Cloud-only changes: %2$d
Direct conflicts: %3$d

Continue?

This will back up current local saves first, then replace local `preferences/` and `saves/` with Steam Cloud. Local changes that are not in Steam Cloud will be overwritten.

Local changes: %1$d
Cloud-only changes: %2$d
Direct conflicts: %3$d

Continue?
A local file path is unsupported and was not added to the upload plan: %1$s
A local file could not be mapped to a Steam Cloud path: %1$s
No previous Steam Cloud sync baseline is saved yet. Files that already exist on both sides will be treated as conflicts until you complete one full pull.

plurals.name: main_steam_cloud_warning_ignored_local_deletions
plurals.item: Ignored %1$d local deletion because Phase 2 uploads do not delete Steam Cloud files.
Ignored %1$d local deletions because Phase 2 uploads do not delete Steam Cloud files.

resources.string: A Steam Cloud file path is unsupported and was ignored: %1$s
Preparing to overwrite Steam Cloud with local progress…
Preparing to overwrite local progress with Steam Cloud…
Steam Cloud now follows local progress: %1$d file(s) uploaded, %2$d remote file(s) removed.
Local saves now follow Steam Cloud: %1$d file(s) applied.
Connect to Steam with the saved refresh token and write the latest cloud manifest locally.
Remove the saved Steam account name and refresh token from this developer-only probe.
Refresh-token mode only. Leave the token blank to keep the saved value.
Refresh-token mode only. The first run requires a saved token.
Proxy is optional. Use formats like `http://127.0.0.1:7897` or `socks5://127.0.0.1:7897`. Android Phase 0 always uses websocket transport.
Refresh-token Android manifest probe is not configured yet.
Ready to run the Android manifest probe.
Last probe: success at %1$s, %2$d files (%3$d preferences, %4$d saves)
Save a Steam account name and refresh token first.
Stored Steam Cloud Phase 0 credentials cleared.
Steam Cloud Phase 0 probe succeeded: %1$d files.
Submit launcher issues, in-game issues, and feature requests. This reporting path is preferred over other channels.
Get new mods, import mods, or export all mods
Share error logs or export error logs
Higher is sharper, lower is lighter on performance.

Only takes effect when the device supports it. When enabled, the launcher requests Sustained Performance Mode while the game is in the foreground. This usually favors long-session stability over higher short spikes. Off by default.

Do not change this unless you know what you are doing. If you hit black screens or similar issues, increasing it may help, but setting it too high can also stop the game from starting. To reduce large-heap startup failures, launch starts with a smaller initial heap and expands on demand.

Controls whether the 64-bit JVM uses compressed object pointers and class pointers. Off by default for compatibility. Enabling it may reduce memory usage, but can also introduce device-specific startup issues.
Controls whether the 64-bit G1GC uses string deduplication. It may reduce heap usage, but adds extra GC CPU overhead.
This mode is controlled by the system Game Dashboard or the vendor game assistant. The launcher cannot change the system mode directly.
If your device supports a game panel, switch it while the game is running from the system panel. Different vendors may call it things like "Game Assistant" or "Game Space".
The launcher currently declares support for Performance and Battery modes. Battery mode may still affect power and scheduling, but the launcher itself does not apply an extra FPS cap.
Map to Esc, useful for pausing the game and similar actions
Controls what happens when you press the system Back button while the game is running.
When enabled, the loading overlay no longer disappears automatically on launch. You must close it manually with the button on the overlay.
Auto-switch back to left click after right click: Enabled
Auto-switch back to left click after right click: Disabled
When enabled, the control mode switches back to left click after one right-click action.
When enabled, the mod card title uses the imported file name.
Controls whether the native mobile UI is enabled. When on, parts of the UI become larger, but some mods may render incorrectly. For example, the Loadout console may disappear.
Uses the entire display area. Ultra-wide screens may stretch the game image.
Fits the game into a 16:9 viewport and uses a 1920x1080 base resolution before render scale is applied.
Fits the game into a 16:9 viewport and uses a 1280x720 base resolution before render scale is applied.
Fits the game into a 4:3 viewport with black bars on wider screens.
Fits the game into a 16:9 viewport with black bars on wider screens.
When enabled, the game avoids displaying important content under the notch or punch-hole area.
When enabled, a black margin is reserved on the right side in landscape to improve taps near the edge on full-screen displays.
Controls whether the real-time FPS / memory overlay in the top-right corner is shown in game.
Controls whether the native touch adaptation mode is used. When disabled, the mouse cursor is shown and the desktop UI is used.
Shows the mouse cursor and uses full desktop click behavior. This has the best click compatibility, but some operations are not suitable for mobile.
Recommended. Keeps the touch input UI and touch indicator, and enables touchscreen logic only for vanilla screens. This can fix cases where mod buttons cannot be tapped under native touch.
Restores global native touchscreen semantics. Closest to vanilla mobile touch behavior, but more likely to trigger mod UI issues.
Uses larger layout sizing. If mods display incorrectly after enabling it, turn this option off.

Controls the low-overhead GPU resource guard for tracked textures and FrameBuffers. Devices with 12 GB RAM or less default to Aggressive; devices above 12 GB default to Safe. Legacy disables the new guardian and enables the locked legacy compatibility paths. This takes effect on the next launch.
Disable guardian and leave resource management to the game/mod stack.
Monitor aggregate tracked bytes with bounded texture/FBO reclaim only after sustained pressure.
Use tighter pressure thresholds and reclaim budgets for severe mod-related GPU memory growth. May cause more rebuild/restore stutter.

Use the most aggressive guardian thresholds and allow reclaiming textures that may require up to 64 MB synchronous restore. This can reduce severe GPU pressure but may cause visible stutter when large mod textures are needed again.
Safe-mode behavior with additional diagnostic logging for short investigations. Do not leave this on for normal play.
Disable the new GPU guardian and enable the legacy locked compatibility paths: runtime texture downscale, texture residency manager, FBO manager, FBO idle reclaim, and FBO pressure downscale.

When the new GPU guardian is enabled, also allow runtime texture and FBO pressure downscale under GPU pressure. This does not rewrite mod jars, but may blur large textures or offscreen effects. Changes apply on the next launch.
Controls org.lwjgl.util.Debug / DebugLoader / DebugFunctions in JVM launch arguments.

Compatibility fallback switch. When enabled, the old behavior is restored and all `.so` files under the runtime directory are preloaded recursively. When disabled, only core JVM libraries and the current renderer dependencies are loaded, which reduces resident native memory.
Mirror JVM logs to logcat: Enabled
Mirror JVM logs to logcat: Disabled
Mirrors appended content from `latest.log` to logcat. Disabled by default.

Controls the extra `[gdx-diag]` per-resource trace logs, stack samples, and large-texture upload diagnostics. The always-on runtime memory summaries and menu-cycle texture owner/source attribution are separate. Disabled by default because this switch increases log volume and a small amount of runtime overhead.
Controls `[gdx-pad-debug]` setCursorPosition output. Disabled by default to reduce log spam.
Controls the swap heartbeat output from GLBridgeDiag. Disabled by default.
Pros: compatibility-oriented; Cons: some devices still need tuning
Pros: closest to the old implementation and stable; Cons: most conservative feature set
Pros: better desktop GL compatibility; Cons: extra translation overhead
Pros: Vulkan path can be steadier on some devices; Cons: requires Vulkan and forces TextureView
Pros: high Mesa/Zink compatibility potential; Cons: highest driver and library requirements
This import will only import into independent saves and will not overwrite cloud saves. To import into cloud saves, use the independent-save-overwrites-cloud-save action on the cloud saves page.

Save import failed: %1$s\n\nIf you are importing a save archive from another launcher build or a third-party source, the failure is likely caused by an incompatible data format and needs manual handling. Export a save archive from this launcher first, open that archive with MT Manager or another archive tool, then replace the exported files with your save files using the same data layout. For example, replace the files in the exported archive preferences folder with the files from the preferences folder you want to import. After replacing all required files, import the modified archive with the launcher.
Android 12 and below do not provide the Game Mode API.
GameManager service is not available on this device.
The system is using the standard game mode. The launcher will run at your configured target FPS.
The system is using performance mode. The launcher will run at your configured target FPS.
The system is using battery mode. The launcher will still use your configured target FPS.
The system is using a custom game mode. The launcher currently treats it like the standard mode.
No usable Game Mode is available for this app on the current system.

The current report is still quite brief, so the developer may not be able to pinpoint the problem accurately. Add symptoms, triggers, and reproduction steps if you can, including your own guesses if helpful.
If you do not provide an email address, you may not receive replies from the developer correctly. It is better to fill in an email before submitting.
Use this form to report launcher bugs, in-game bugs, and feature requests. Logs, device info, and the enabled mod snapshot are attached automatically, and you can also add screenshots.
This build has no feedback upload endpoint configured. You can fill in the form, but it cannot actually submit to the cloud function.
1. What do you want to report?
Choose the closest type first, then continue to the detailed form.
Includes game crashes, display problems, and mods not working correctly in game.
Problems outside the game, for example when launcher mod categories stop working.
Ask for a new feature, for example the ability to rename mods in the launcher.
Yes, this happened in the most recent run
No, but I still want to submit it
It is better to reproduce once more so the logs are more complete, but you can still continue.
If you cannot narrow it down, at least select this option.
There are no enabled mods right now, so the report will only include logs and environment details.
When filling out this section, describe the issue in as much detail and with as much precision as possible so developers can locate and fix it accurately.

Describe the problem in detail: which mod seems responsible, what you think is happening, what you have already tried, and any other facts you already know. The more detail you provide, the easier it is for the developer to solve.
Screenshots are optional. You can attach up to 4. Prefer the broken screen, crash popup, or the frozen scene.
Send me an email when this report gets an update.
Your email address will not be shown publicly in the GitHub Issue.
I understand that confusing issue descriptions will slow down the resolution process.
I understand that the launcher will not provide fixes for conflicts caused between mods.
I confirm that before submitting this feedback, I already tried to fix the problem, but still could not solve it, or only partially located the cause.
I understand that the developer is not customer support and is not obligated to solve problems for me.
I understand that the developer has probably never played the mod that is failing, and my description is clear enough for someone unfamiliar with it to understand.
I have carefully read all the instructions, and I should not check this checkbox.
Every issue takes substantial effort to locate. Confirm the basic notices first so neither side wastes time.
This page lists the issues you already follow. You can check progress, open the conversation, or unfollow them here.
You are not following any issues yet.
This list shows repository issues from newest update to oldest. You can also filter by status. When you find one you want to keep tracking, tap Follow.
There are no issues to show right now.
No issues match the current filter yet. Load more or switch the filter.
No issues match the current filter.
All currently visible issues have been loaded.
Example: remember the last email used in the feedback form
Example: the launcher crashes right after importing a certain mod
Example: large black blocks appear on the main menu
Example: the game turns black and crashes after a card is played
Example: something goes wrong after entering the game
Choose the report type first, then fill in the summary
Explain why you need this feature and what pain point it solves.
Describe what you did in the launcher, what you saw, and what felt wrong.
Example: performance keeps stuttering after entering a battle with Card Library Master enabled.
Example: many black blocks appear on the main menu after using Ring of Fate.
Example: the game turns black and crashes after a certain card is played by a modded character.
Briefly describe the in-game problem you ran into.
Choose the report type first, then fill in the details
Example: I frequently switch between different mod sets and want to avoid filling this in repeatedly.
List the steps in order. The more specific, the better.
Close this issue from the launcher?
Reopen this issue from the launcher?
Write what you want to add for the developer.
You can attach up to %1$d screenshots.
Screenshot limit reached. Only the first %1$d were kept.
This build has no feedback upload endpoint configured, so submission is currently unavailable.
Fill in the one-line summary first.
Enter an email address first if you want email updates.
The email format does not look valid.
Describe the feature you want added.
First decide whether this happened in the most recent run.
Select the mods you suspect, or choose "Not sure".
Check all required acknowledgement items first.

Feedback submitted. GitHub Issue #%1$d was created and automatically added to Tracked issues.\n\nYou can follow it later from Settings -> Feedback -> Tracked issues. When there are updates, the launcher home screen will also show a notification.

Feedback submitted. GitHub Issue #%1$d was created and automatically added to Tracked issues.\n\nTracked issues are limited to 10 entries, so the oldest tracked issue, Issue #%2$d, was removed automatically.
Feedback submitted. GitHub Issue #%1$d was created.\n\nYou can follow it later from Settings -> Feedback -> Tracked issues.
Feedback submitted. The Issue has been created.
Feedback submitted. The cloud function has received the request.
Now following Issue #%1$d. You can continue tracking it from Tracked issues.
Now following Issue #%1$d. Tracked issues are limited to 10 entries, so the oldest tracked issue, Issue #%2$d, was removed automatically.
Enter a message or attach screenshots first.
On first launch, you need to import `desktop-1.0.jar` before entering the main screen.
Choose how to prepare the game files for this launcher.
Already downloaded desktop-1.0.jar; import it directly, then log in to Steam later.
After login, download game files from Steam and enable full cloud save and Workshop support.
Keep this page open while desktop-1.0.jar is downloaded and imported automatically.
Changing this during download pauses the current request, switches source, then resumes downloading.
The current download will be paused, then resumed through the acceleration link.
The current download will be paused, then resumed through the official Steam source.
Waiting for Steam to return file information...
`desktop-1.0.jar` comes from the PC installation directory of Slay the Spire.
1. Copy `desktop-1.0.jar` from the game install directory on your PC
1 tracked issue has a new update.
Failed to save larger UI setting: %1$s
Import atlas pre-downscale level: max edge %1$dpx
These options only take effect when the MobileGlues graphics backend is active. Any change on this screen can cause rendering problems, so only change them if you understand the tradeoffs.

Left favors performance, right favors compatibility. Higher compatibility is not guaranteed to be better; conservative strategies can also trigger new issues. Try every preset and keep the one that fits your device best.
When enabled, all MobileGlues-specific options are shown. When disabled, only the four presets and the current parameter summary remain.
Controls whether ANGLE is used. Mate 70 / Maleoon devices should try Force enabled first.
If Auto picks an overly aggressive path, try Force draw elements instead.
The more aggressive this is, the easier it is to hide the real graphics issue.
Used to narrow or widen the desktop GL capability range exposed to the game.
This is fairly experimental. Some shaders or mods may choose more aggressive paths because of it.
Usually not required. Keep it disabled if you are troubleshooting driver issues.
Provides a more modern resource access path for some desktop GL flows.
Only meaningful when the ANGLE path is active.
This only controls the preset and does not delete old cache files automatically.
May improve performance, or may introduce image artifacts.
This screen is intended for advanced users only. Changing options here may stop the game from launching, make it crash, or leave it on a black screen. Change them only if you know what you are doing.

Minimizes translation and draw overhead first. Best for devices with stable drivers and a strong focus on frame rate. This is the riskiest preset; if you see black screens, crashes, or corruption, move further right.
Keeps relatively high performance while dialing back part of the aggressive setup. Suitable for most devices with normal graphics driver behavior.
Prioritizes stability and starts converging toward safer graphics paths. Suitable for devices with occasional crashes, shader errors, or black screens.
Converges as far as possible toward conservative paths. Suitable for Huawei / Maleoon or other known unstable devices. This is the slowest preset, but the easiest way to get the game running first.
Prefer not to use ANGLE and only enable it automatically when it improves compatibility.
Recommended default. Prefer ANGLE, but still allow automatic fallback depending on the device.
Never use ANGLE regardless of device behavior.
Always use ANGLE regardless of device behavior.
Let MobileGlues decide whether graphics errors should be ignored.
Keep full error checks. This is the most conservative compatibility choice.
Swallows some shader compile and program link errors. Enable it only when you clearly need it.
Ignore shader, program, and framebuffer errors
The most aggressive option. It can hide the real problem.
Let MobileGlues choose automatically based on driver capability.
Prefer the glDrawElementsIndirect path and fall back automatically if it fails.
Prefer the base-vertex path. Useful for some older drivers.
The most aggressive automatic batch submission path.
The most conservative option, usually with the best compatibility and the lowest speed.
Uses the compute path. This is experimental.
Do not keep a GLSL cache. Uses the least space.
A small cache, suitable for conservative testing.
A medium cache for general use.
A more aggressive shader cache preset.
The largest preset, focused on reducing repeated compilation.
Disabled by default. Do not override ANGLE depth clear behavior.
A relatively conservative ANGLE depth clear fix.
A more aggressive fix. Try it only if #1 does not work.
Let MobileGlues use its default OpenGL version.
The highest version and the most aggressive choice.
Suitable for testing newer desktop GL paths.
Keeps newer features while usually being slightly safer.
Closest to the current default experience.
More conservative and often useful for driver investigation.
The most conservative desktop GL preset.
Do not enable the built-in FSR1.
Best image quality with the lightest sharpening.
A common balance between image quality and performance.
The most aggressive choice and may introduce visible artifacts.
View Workshop mods subscribed by the current Steam account
View Workshop download progress and task details
This Steam account has not subscribed to any Slay the Spire Workshop mods.
Steam is not signed in. Some mods may not appear.
Sign in to Steam to view Workshop mods subscribed by the current account.
%1$s requires the following missing dependencies. Add them to the download queue too?
Try another keyword, or refresh Steam Workshop later.
Continuing will restart this task. Resume download is not supported by the backend yet.
Choose export to file, or share this download log through the system share sheet.
After you select a mod in Workshop and tap download, tasks will appear here.
Download completed. Install it from the Mods page.
Workshop update check completed, %1$d checks failed
Workshop mods are up to date
Steam comments cannot be read right now.
No changelog is available for this mod.
This mod has no title or description to translate.
Translation failed. Please try again later.
Baidu AI text translation AppID and API Key are not configured. Configure them in Settings > Market first.
Baidu AI text translation AppID is not configured.
Baidu AI text translation API Key is not configured.
Baidu AI text translation credentials are invalid. Check AppID and API Key.
Comments are loading. 5 comments are shown per page.
This mod has no public comments yet.
Folders cannot be changed right now. Try again after the main process is ready.
Not enabled or not signed in
Sign in to Steam and enable cloud saves in Settings to show sync status here.
This mod was downloaded from the market and will be checked for updates automatically.
This will download again and overwrite the current installation of %1$s. Do not start the game while updating.
